zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I於1165年出生在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛時，他的父親William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough83歲，母親Ermentrude Ferreres23歲。他於大約1185年與Ermentrude de Ferrers在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1225年在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛 哥吉拉 沙智享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I于1165年出生在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛时，他的父亲William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough83岁，母亲Ermentrude Ferreres23岁。他于大约1185年与Ermentrude de Ferrers在英国英格兰林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1225年在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛 哥吉拉 沙智享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Talbot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Marcel, Anatole, Armand, Aubert, Camille, Gilles, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Auguste.
English and Irish (of Norman origin); French: from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Talebot, a pet form of Old French Talebert, Talbert (see Talbert 1). Compare Talbert 2.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Talbald, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + bald ‘bold’.

variants.name: Talbert
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin); French: from the Old French personal name Talebert, Talbert, from ancient Germanic Talaberht or Dalbert, composed of the elements dal ‘bright, proud’ or tal ‘valley’ + berht ‘bright’.
Altered form of English   Talbot  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Talbott
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Talbot  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I was born in 1165, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, his father, William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough, was 83 and his mother, Ermentrude Ferreres, was 23. He married Ermentrude de Ferrers about 1185, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1225, in Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I est né en 1165, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough, avait 83 ans et sa mère, Ermentrude Ferreres, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Ermentrude de Ferrers environ 1185, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1225, à Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I 1165 in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough 83 und seine Mutter Ermentrude Ferreres 23. Er hat um 1185 in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Ermentrude de Ferrers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1225 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I è nato il nel 1165 nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough, aveva 83 anni e il suo madre, Ermentrude Ferreres, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Ermentrude de Ferrers circa 1185, nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1225, nel Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot Iが1165年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウで生まれた時，父William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsboroughは83歳，母Ermentrude Ferreresは23歳であった。約1185年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウでErmentrude de Ferrersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1225年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウ アル サインツで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I(이)가 1165년 영국 링코실르 게인스버루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough(은)는 83세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermentrude Ferreres(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1185년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 게인스버루에서 Ermentrude de Ferrers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1225년 영국 링코실르 게인스버루 앨 세인츠에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I nasceu em 1165, em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough, tinha 83 anos e sua mãe, Ermentrude Ferreres, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Ermentrude de Ferrers aproximadamente 1185, em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1225, em Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I родился в 1165 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough было 83 года, а матери, которую звали Ermentrude Ferreres, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Ermentrude de Ferrers около 1185 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1225 г. в местности геинсбороую алл сеинц, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert ‘Quinton’ de Talbot I nació en 1165, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Talbot III   Lord Of Gainsborough, tenía 83 años y su mamá, Ermentrude Ferreres, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Ermentrude de Ferrers aproximadamente 1185, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1225, en Gainsborough All Saints, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años.
